comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Fix implemented + live-verified in PR #36 (auto-merge armed). Outbound: _mutation_to_batch_dict now surfaces links + update_one dispatches set_relationship with dedup probe. Inbound: new get_issuelinks_map + fetcher enrichment (fixes inbound visibility AND outbound re-emission churn). RED->GREEN unit tests in test_link_sync.py; full reconciler suite 1004 passed. Live on REB-413/414/415 (deleted): blocks both directions now sync, idempotent, parent regression-clean.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
**Completion Verification: PASS**

All five acceptance criteria are met by the implementation. Evidence:

**AC1 — Outbound: local `blocks`/`relates_to` dep creates Jira link (no silent no-op)**
- `batch_dispatch._mutation_to_batch_dict` now surfaces `"links"` key (`batch_dispatch.py:657`).
- `batch_dispatch.update_one` dispatches via `client.set_relationship` with a live dedup probe (`batch_dispatch.py:522–559`).
- `apply_outbound._apply_outbound_update` also handles links and increments `links_applied` (`apply_outbound.py:325–396`).
- Unit tests: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/apply/test_applier_outbound_links.py` (8 tests, no xfail/skip).

**AC2 — Inbound: Jira Blocks/Relates link mirrored to local dep**
- New `get_issuelinks_map` (`acli_graph.py:391–482`) amortises issuelink fetch into one paged REST search.
- `fetcher.py:441–475` enriches the snapshot with `issuelinks` per bound issue.
- `inbound_differ._diff_links_inbound` (`inbound_differ.py:440–514`) reads `jira_fields.get("issuelinks")` and emits link mutations.
- Unit tests: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/apply/test_applier_inbound_links.py` (including real-store observable test).

**AC3 — Outbound link re-emission churn stops**
- `outbound_differ._existing_jira_links` (`outbound_differ.py:957–980`) dedups against `issuelinks`; since the snapshot now carries `issuelinks`, the dedup fires correctly and no link is re-emitted if already present.
- Tests: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_link_dedup.py` pins the "already present → no re-ADD" contract for both directions.

**AC4 — Parent-child regression guard**
- Pre-existing tests `test_inbound_differ_bidir_coordination.py` and `test_inbound_differ_field_contract.py` cover the `parent`→`parent_id` canonicalization path unchanged by this fix.

**AC5 — RED→GREEN tests for both directions**
- `test_applier_outbound_links.py`, `test_applier_inbound_links.py`, `tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_link_sync.py` (A1 outbound + A2 inbound integration tests), `test_link_dedup.py`. No `xfail`/`skip` markers found on any of these.

**Bug resolution:** The two root causes are addressed — `batch_dispatch._mutation_to_batch_dict` no longer drops the `links` field, `update_one` now calls `set_relationship`, and the snapshot is enriched with `issuelinks` so both `_diff_links_inbound` and `_existing_jira_links` have real Jira link data to work with.

compiled_state.description:
## Summary

Live testing of relationship sync (REB-410/411/412 throwaway tickets, since deleted) found that **issue-link relationships (blocks/depends_on/relates_to) do not sync in EITHER direction**, while **parent-child sync works both ways**. Two independent root causes, mirroring previously-fixed bugs in the same files.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Two bound tickets A, B.
2. **Outbound:** `rebar link A B blocks`; run `rebar reconcile --mode live`.
3. **Inbound:** create a Blocks link A->B directly in Jira (`acli jira workitem link create --out A --in B --type Blocks --yes`); run reconcile.

## Expected vs Actual

- **Expected:** outbound — a Jira Blocks link A->B appears; inbound — a local `blocks` dep appears on A.
- **Actual (outbound):** **no Jira link created**, yet the pass reports `links=1` + `batch_outcome ... error=None` + `OK: applied`. Silent no-op (re-emitted every pass — the fleet-wide `links=N` churn on REB-1, REB-8, REB-9, ... is this).
- **Actual (inbound):** **no local dep created** (`inbound_differ ... ` never sees the link).

## Root cause (confirmed in code)

- **Outbound:** production outbound dispatch routes through the **batch path** (`batch_dispatch.update_one`), NOT the typed leaf `apply_outbound._apply_outbound_update` (which DOES handle links). `batch_dispatch._mutation_to_batch_dict` surfaces `comments` and `labels` but **omits `links`** (batch_dispatch.py ~579-590), and `update_one` has **no link dispatch** at all. So the link entry is dropped before any `set_relationship` call. (`set_relationship` itself works — verified by calling it directly.) Same class as the parent bug 8b25 and comment bug 87e4 fixed in this same path.
- **Inbound:** `inbound_differ._diff_links_inbound` reads `jira_fields.get('issuelinks')`, but the snapshot **never carries `issuelinks`** — the fetcher enriches `parent` (get_parent_map) and `comment` (get_comment_map) but has **no issuelinks enrichment**, and the base search omits it. So the inbound link differ is structurally blind. Exact same class as the inbound-comment bug (0ee6, fixed): snapshot omits the field.

## Fix approach

- Outbound: add `links` to `_mutation_to_batch_dict`'s output and dispatch them in `update_one` via `client.set_relationship` (mirror the typed leaf's dedup-probe + link_errors handling), OR route outbound updates through the typed leaf.
- Inbound: enrich the snapshot with `issuelinks` (a get_issuelinks_map mirroring get_comment_map/get_parent_map), so `_diff_links_inbound` sees real Jira links.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A local `blocks`/`relates_to` dep on a bound ticket creates the corresponding Jira link on the next pass (no silent no-op; `links_applied` reflects reality).
- [ ] A Blocks/Relates link created in Jira is mirrored to a local dep on the next pass.
- [ ] Outbound link re-emission churn stops (links converge; no perpetual `links=N` every pass).
- [ ] Parent-child sync remains working in both directions (regression guard).
- [ ] RED->GREEN tests for both directions.
compiled_state.title: Issue-link (blocks/relates) relationship sync is broken in BOTH directions
